title: Gomesa carlosregentii

Within this family, it is further classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The tribe and genus classifications for Gomesa carlosregentii are Cymbidieae and Gomesa, respectively. It is a relatively rare species within its genus.

intro: Gomesa carlosregentii, commonly known as the 'Prince Regent's Gomesa', is an orchid species native to the tropical regions of South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Gomesa carlosregentii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gomesa carlosregentii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
